---
title: "MailinMarylandFreq"
author: "Isabelle Jensen"
date: "2026-02-12"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(data.table)
library(knitr)
library(lubridate)
library(janitor)
library(gmodels)
library(gt)
library(kableExtra)
library(ggplot2)

library(readxl)

mailin <- read_excel("Mail-inList.xlsx")

glimpse(mailin)

table(mailin$AB_APP_STATUS)
table(mailin$AB_TYPE)
table(mailin$BALLOT_SENT_DATE)
table(Responded = !is.na(mailin$BALLOT_SENT_DATE))
table(Responded = !is.na(mailin$BALLOT_RETURNED_DATE))

table(
  Sent = !is.na(mailin$BALLOT_SENT_DATE),
  Returned = !is.na(mailin$BALLOT_RETURNED_DATE)
)

mailin$days_to_return <- as.numeric(
  mailin$BALLOT_RETURNED_DATE - mailin$BALLOT_SENT_DATE,
  units = "days"
)

valid <- !is.na(mailin$days_to_return)
summary(mailin$days_to_return[valid])
sd(mailin$days_to_return[valid])

mailin$days_to_return <- as.numeric(
  mailin$BALLOT_RETURNED_DATE - mailin$BALLOT_SENT_DATE,
  units = "days"
)
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(mailin, aes(x = days_to_return)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, na.rm = TRUE) +
  labs(
    title = "Days Between Ballot Sent and Returned",
    x = "Days to Return Ballot",
    y = "Number of Voters"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()



sum(mailin$days_to_return < 0, na.rm = TRUE)
#795 people returned ballot before it was sent

summary(mailin$days_to_return[mailin$days_to_return < 0])


mailin$days_to_return_clean <- ifelse(mailin$days_to_return < 0, NA, mailin$days_to_return)


valid <- !is.na(mailin$days_to_return_clean)
sum(valid)

c(
  N = sum(valid),
  Mean = mean(mailin$days_to_return_clean[valid]),
  Median = median(mailin$days_to_return_clean[valid]),
  SD = sd(mailin$days_to_return_clean[valid]),
  Min = min(mailin$days_to_return_clean[valid]),
  Max = max(mailin$days_to_return_clean[valid])
)



ggplot(mailin, aes(x = days_to_return_clean)) +
  geom_density(na.rm = TRUE) +
  labs(
    title = "Density of Ballot Return Timing (No Negatives)",
    x = "Days to Return Ballot After Sent",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()



```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r web request}
glimpse(mailin$ABSENTEE_ADDRESS)
glimpse(mailin$BALLOT_RETURNED_DATE)

library(dplyr)

mailin <- mailin %>%
  mutate(
    web_delivery = if_else(ABSENTEE_ADDRESS == "WEB DELIVERY", 1, 0),
    ballot_returned = if_else(!is.na(BALLOT_RETURNED_DATE), 1, 0)
  )


table(mailin$web_delivery, mailin$ballot_returned)

prop.table(
  table(mailin$web_delivery, mailin$ballot_returned),
  margin = 1
)





library(dplyr)

mailin <- mailin %>%
  mutate(
    web_delivery = if_else(ABSENTEE_ADDRESS == "WEB DELIVERY", 1, 0),
    ballot_returned = if_else(!is.na(BALLOT_RETURNED_DATE), 1, 0),
    
    web_delivery_lab = factor(
      web_delivery,
      levels = c(1, 0),
      labels = c("Web Delivery", "Other")
    ),
    
    ballot_returned_lab = factor(
      ballot_returned,
      levels = c(1, 0),
      labels = c("Ballot Returned", "Not Returned")
    )
  )



tab <- table(mailin$web_delivery_lab,
             mailin$ballot_returned_lab)

tab



prop.table(tab, margin = 1)


model1 <- glm(
  ballot_returned ~ web_delivery,
  data = mailin,
  family = binomial(link = "logit")
)

summary(model1)



glimpse(mailin$BALLOT_STATUS)



mailin <- mailin %>%
  mutate(
    web_delivery = if_else(ABSENTEE_ADDRESS == "WEB DELIVERY", 1, 0),
    accepted = if_else(BALLOT_STATUS == "Accepted", 1, 0)
  )


model_status <- glm(
  accepted ~ web_delivery,
  data = mailin,
  family = binomial(link = "logit")
)

summary(model_status)



library(dplyr)

mailin <- mailin %>%
  mutate(
    web_delivery = if_else(ABSENTEE_ADDRESS == "WEB DELIVERY", 1, 0),
    accepted = if_else(BALLOT_STATUS == "Accepted", 1, 0),
    
    web_delivery_lab = factor(
      web_delivery,
      levels = c(1, 0),
      labels = c("Web Delivery", "Other")
    ),
    
    accepted_lab = factor(
      accepted,
      levels = c(1, 0),
      labels = c("Accepted", "Not Accepted")
    )
  )


mailin %>%
  tabyl(web_delivery_lab, accepted_lab) %>%
  adorn_percentages("row") %>%
  adorn_pct_formatting(digits = 1) %>%
  adorn_ns(position = "front")




library(dplyr)

mailin <- mailin %>%
  mutate(
    web_delivery = if_else(
      ABSENTEE_ADDRESS == "WEB DELIVERY",
      1, 0, 
      missing = 0
    ),
    
    accepted = if_else(
      BALLOT_STATUS == "Accepted",
      1, 0,
      missing = 0
    ),
    
    web_delivery_lab = factor(
      web_delivery,
      levels = c(1, 0),
      labels = c("Web Delivery", "Other")
    ),
    
    accepted_lab = factor(
      accepted,
      levels = c(1, 0),
      labels = c("Accepted", "Not Accepted")
    )
  )


library(janitor)

mailin %>%
  tabyl(web_delivery_lab, accepted_lab) %>%
  adorn_percentages("row") %>%
  adorn_pct_formatting(digits = 1) %>%
  adorn_ns(position = "front")

table(mailin$web_delivery_lab)



```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
